summary: Two deadlines now imminent: Copilot Studio classic agent creation retires June 30 (tomorrow) and Cowork usage-based billing via Copilot Credits begins July 1 with Cowork off by default — escalating Day 2 and Day 3 materials/labs; M365 Copilot's new chat-first UI, Claude model picker, Copilot Notebooks, and FLUX.2 keep Day 1 Session 2 materials outdated.

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Short promotional trailer; content is framing-level and unlikely to reference specific feature details that have changed.

materials.title: Manager Kickoff Presentation
materials.finding: With Cowork usage-based billing (Copilot Credits) going live July 1, managers now need to understand that Cowork is off by default and consumes credits per task — a budgeting consideration the deck likely omits. Work Trend Index 2026 also reframes Copilot from assistant to agent-first coworker.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a short slide on the Cowork Copilot Credits model (effective July 1) and what managers should know about enabling it and setting spend caps for their teams. Refresh the Copilot value framing to agent-first language.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator talking points may reference Copilot as an AI assistant rather than the current agent-first positioning, and likely do not prepare managers for the July 1 Cowork credits/billing conversation with their teams.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a talking point that Cowork must be admin-enabled and is usage-billed from July 1. Align capability framing with the agent-first messaging from Work Trend Index 2026.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video talk track may use outdated terminology (AI assistant vs. AI agent) and does not reference the Cowork usage-based billing model that managers need to understand for team budget planning ahead of the July 1 start.
materials.suggestedAction: Add supplemental notes covering the Copilot Credits billing model and agent-first framing if re-recording is not feasible.

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Motivational/icebreaker content; not dependent on specific product features.

materials.title: Presentation Deck
materials.finding: The AI-first mindset framing should reflect the Work Trend Index 2026 thesis that organizational redesign drives more value than individual AI use (67% vs 32%), the rise of 'Frontier Firms,' and 15x year-over-year growth in active M365 agents. The deck's framing may still position AI as an assistant rather than an agent-first operating model.
materials.suggestedAction: Refresh the AI-first mindset slides with Work Trend Index 2026 data points (Frontier Firms, organizational readiness, agent-first paradigm) to strengthen the opening narrative.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Guide talking points about the AI-first operating model may not yet reflect the agent-first shift and 'Frontier Firm' framing emphasized in the 2026 Work Trend Index. Discussion prompts and examples should reference current Microsoft positioning.
materials.suggestedAction: Update discussion prompts and facilitator notes to reference the agent-first paradigm and the organizational-redesign emphasis of Work Trend Index 2026.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video talk track establishes AI-first mindset framing. May reference older AI positioning that predates the 2026 Work Trend Index and the agent-first / Frontier Firm shift.
materials.suggestedAction: Review video for alignment with current agent-first messaging. If it frames AI only as a productivity booster, add supplemental notes bridging to the agent-as-coworker framing.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 1 — M365 Copilot: From Overwhelmed to In-Command

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Short promotional teaser; general framing unlikely to be impacted by specific feature changes.

materials.title: Presentation Deck
materials.finding: M365 Copilot's June 2026 redesign is now broadly rolled out: new chat-first unified UI (also in Edge), a model picker (GPT-5.5 Instant vs. Claude Opus 4.8) with Quick/Think Deep modes, Copilot Notebooks, infinite scroll in chat history, FLUX.2 image generation in PowerPoint, and federated connectors now GA. Screenshots and feature walkthroughs in this deck no longer match the live product.
materials.suggestedAction: Replace all UI screenshots with the new chat-first interface. Add slides on model selection (Claude vs GPT-5.5 Instant, Quick/Think Deep), Copilot Notebooks, and live/federated connectors. Note FLUX.2 image generation where image creation is shown.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator demo instructions reference the pre-June 2026 Copilot UI and feature set. The new chat-first interface, model picker, and Copilot Notebooks change how facilitators should demonstrate the tool, and the talk-through sequence will not match what participants see on screen.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite demo steps for the chat-first UI. Add facilitator guidance on when to recommend Claude vs GPT-5.5 Instant and how to use Quick/Think Deep. Insert a Copilot Notebooks walkthrough into the feature sequence.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video shows the pre-June 2026 Copilot UI and demo flow. The new chat-first interface, model picker, and Copilot Notebooks are not represented, so facilitators following this video will demonstrate an interface participants won't see.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record the talk track on the current chat-first Copilot UI, including a model-selection demo and a Copilot Notebooks walkthrough, or produce a short supplemental clip covering only the changed UI elements.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises that walk participants through M365 Copilot use outdated navigation steps. The chat-first interface changes how Copilot features are accessed, model selection (Claude/GPT-5.5 Instant) is now an explicit step, and Copilot Notebooks may better fit information-organization exercises.
materials.suggestedAction: Update all step-by-step instructions to the chat-first UI. Add guidance on choosing a model for each lab task and consider incorporating Copilot Notebooks where participants organize information.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 2 — Copilot Cowork: Delegate, Don't Dictate

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Teaser promotes the concept of delegation; the Cowork GA reinforces this message rather than contradicting it.

materials.title: Presentation Deck
materials.finding: Cowork GA (June 16) capabilities now apply, and usage-based billing via Copilot Credits begins July 1 (2 days out) with Cowork off by default. The deck likely lacks the credits/billing model and the GA feature set: browser automation in Edge, model choice (GPT-5.5, Claude Opus/Sonnet), Imagen 2 image generation, brand templates, and the single Customize page for plugins/skills.
materials.suggestedAction: Add slides on the Copilot Credits billing model and cost management (effective July 1) and the fact that Cowork is admin-enabled/off by default. Update feature demos to show browser automation, model selection, image generation, and brand templates; replace any preview disclaimers with GA positioning.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Demo scripts were likely written for the Frontier preview. GA changes the setup (Cowork off by default, admin-enabled), introduces the Copilot Credits billing model effective July 1, and adds browser automation and an expanded plugin catalog. Critically, if a tenant hasn't enabled usage-based billing by July 1, Cowork stops working — a prerequisite facilitators must verify before any live demo.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite the demo flow for GA. Add a prerequisite check that the tenant has Cowork enabled and billing configured before July 1. Add talking points on Copilot Credits, spend caps, and cost visibility, plus a browser-automation demo sequence.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video demonstrates the preview-era Cowork interface and capabilities. The GA version has a new UI, model picker, browser automation, and a usage-based pricing model, so facilitators following this video will show flows that no longer match production.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record the video on the GA Cowork interface, demonstrating model selection, browser automation, brand templates, and the Copilot Credits cost indicators, and show the admin enablement flow relevant to facilitator prep.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Cowork lab exercises reference preview-era capabilities and UI. With usage-based billing starting July 1 and Cowork off by default, participants will be unable to run lab tasks unless the tenant has Cowork enabled and credits provisioned — and they will now see credit consumption. GA also adds a model-selection step and browser-automation tasks.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a setup prerequisite confirming Cowork is enabled with credits before the lab. Rewrite navigation for the GA UI, add model-selection and browser-automation exercises, and include a short section on monitoring Copilot Credits usage.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 3 — Build Your Own Copilot: No Code Required

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Promotional teaser focused on empowerment messaging; the no-code concept remains valid even with UI changes.

materials.title: Presentation Deck
materials.finding: Copilot Studio's classic agent creation in Teams retires June 30 — tomorrow — after which all creation must occur in the web app via the new Unified Workflows Canvas. The deck's screenshots and creation walkthrough show the old separate authoring experience and any Teams-based creation path, and miss Computer-Using Agents (GA), agent memory, modular skills, and real-time voice.
materials.suggestedAction: Urgent: remove all Teams-based agent-creation references before Day 3 delivery. Replace screenshots with the Unified Workflows Canvas and update the no-code walkthrough to the merged canvas. Add Computer-Using Agents, agent memory, and modular skills as new capabilities.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Demo instructions reference the old Copilot Studio authoring experience. The Unified Workflows Canvas changes navigation and creation flow, and any instructions pointing participants to Teams-based creation will fail starting June 30.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite all demo steps for the Unified Workflows Canvas and remove Teams-based creation references entirely. Update prerequisites to specify web-app access and add facilitator notes on Computer-Using Agents and agent memory.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video shows the pre-Unified-Canvas Copilot Studio interface. The agent-creation workflow has changed to the merged drag-and-drop canvas, and the Teams creation path retires June 30, so facilitators following this video will demonstrate an interface that no longer exists.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record the video on the Unified Workflows Canvas in the web app, demonstrating the merged authoring experience and Computer-Using Agents as an advanced no-code example.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises that build agents in Copilot Studio use the old creation flow. The Unified Workflows Canvas changes all step-by-step instructions, and any lab steps that use Teams-based creation will break starting June 30 — a hard blocker for Day 3 labs delivered on or after that date.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite all lab steps for the Unified Workflows Canvas in the web app (no Teams path). Verify every navigation path and button label against the current GA interface and add an optional Computer-Using Agents exercise.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 4 — Work Redesign: Rethinking the Work Itself

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Conceptual teaser about work redesign; the theme is reinforced by current Microsoft messaging.

materials.title: Presentation Deck
materials.finding: Work Trend Index 2026 strongly supports this session's thesis: organizational redesign drives ~2x more AI value than individual use, and 66% of AI users report spending more time on high-value work. The deck's argument aligns with current direction but could be reinforced with these data points and the 'Frontier Firm' framing.
materials.suggestedAction: Add Work Trend Index 2026 data points to strengthen the work-redesign argument and reference the shift from 'making work faster' to redesigning work around AI agents.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator discussion prompts and examples could benefit from Work Trend Index 2026 data. The session's core thesis about rethinking work (not just speeding it up) is strongly validated by current Microsoft research.
materials.suggestedAction: Add facilitator talking points referencing Work Trend Index 2026 findings and update discussion examples to reference agent-first work patterns and Frontier Firm transformation cases.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video talk track covers work-redesign concepts that remain directionally correct but may miss the latest Work Trend Index 2026 data and the acceleration of agent-first thinking since Build 2026.
materials.suggestedAction: Review the video for opportunities to add supplemental notes about Work Trend Index 2026 data as discussion enrichment.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Work-redesign lab exercises are conceptual/strategic and less dependent on specific UI. However, if exercises reference Copilot capabilities for task automation, they should reflect the current tool landscape (Cowork GA with credits-based usage from July 1, Copilot Notebooks, agent-first patterns).
materials.suggestedAction: Review lab scenarios so any hands-on Copilot steps reference current capabilities (Cowork for delegation, Notebooks for organization) and note the July 1 Cowork billing change if exercises rely on Cowork.
